fields.description:
## Context

rebar's authenticated-authorship capability is fully built and merged (identity epic
gnu-whale-ichor / e68d, foundation 2fd4, op-cert asymmetric 6d0d), and the HMAC->sshsig
attestation migration completed cleanly on 2026-07-14. But the LAST MILE — turning on
event-authorship signing so `identity.require_authenticated` can be flipped to `true` —
was deferred out of every build epic into the parked idea a422 (dated-circular-velvetworm)
and never executed. Post-mortem findings this epic remediates:

- **Local sessions/worktrees on this machine** have no persistent signing key (no
  `identity.signing_key`, no `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`, no `.rebar/current_identity`);
  only ~4 one-off validation-demo signed events exist store-wide vs ~5,000 unsigned.
- **GitHub Actions** (`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`) already has the full
  key-materialize + job-level env wiring (story 9e76/cc0b) but is secret-gated: with
  `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` unset it writes UNSIGNED (advisory). Exactly one CI-authored
  signed event exists store-wide.
- **Gerrit-side operations** (review-bot signing 245e, reconciler inline-sign 9e76,
  opcert-service) landed in code, but the post-deploy validation canary 1787 was
  FORCE-CLOSED "pending post-deploy run" — never confirmed to sign on the deployed VM.
- **The guardrail** bug 27ae made the merge-gate config-driven and set an `enforce_since`
  grandfather boundary; the perf blocker 1cc0 is fixed. Flipping the flag today would fail
  every push because the store is unsigned — the flip must land AFTER every writer signs,
  in the same change that pins `enforce_since`.

This epic supersedes a422 and drives the rollout to the point where
`identity.require_authenticated = true` can be enabled with NOTHING failing on any writer
surface.

## Scope / planned child stories

1. **Local machine signing** — provision a persistent signing key for local
   worktrees/sessions (per-machine `identity.signing_key` or exported
   `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`), register/confirm the identity, verify steady-state
   signed events across a fresh session.
2. **GitHub Actions signing** — populate the `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret and confirm
   `reconcile-bridge` (and its canary) produce signed events on a real run.
3. **Gerrit-side signing** — finish canary 1787: confirm the deployed review-bot /
   reconciler / opcert-service signs its ticket writes on the VM.
4. **Grandfathering remediation** — verify/repin `identity.enforce_since` to the
   flip-cutover commit and confirm `verify-identity` grandfathers all pre-boundary events;
   add/confirm a regression test around the boundary.
5. **The atomic flip** — set `identity.require_authenticated = true` + the pinned
   `enforce_since` in ONE change; confirm the merge-gate enforces going forward.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Every writer environment (this machine, GitHub Actions, Gerrit-side services)
      produces SIGNED events in steady state, confirmed by `rebar verify-identity`
      reporting them `verified`.
- [ ] The rollout is sequenced safely: signing is confirmed on every surface BEFORE the
      enforcement flag is flipped (so the gate never goes red on an unsigned writer).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **With `identity.require_authenticated = true`, no writer surface fails.** A
      representative ticket write from each of (a) a fresh local session/worktree on this
      machine, (b) a GitHub Actions run that writes tickets (`reconcile-bridge`), and (c) a
      Gerrit-side operation that writes tickets (review-bot / reconciler / opcert-service)
      each produces a signed event and is accepted; the `rebar verify-identity` merge-gate
      exits 0 for all three. Proof: run a write on each surface and show
      `authorship: {signed: >=1}` + a `verified` verdict; no CI job goes red and no local
      write is rejected.
- [ ] **Pre-flip events are grandfathered.** `identity.enforce_since` is pinned to the
      enforcement-cutover commit; `rebar verify-identity` over the full history exits 0 with
      zero pre-boundary events flagged. Proof: a synthetic UNSIGNED event dated before the
      boundary passes the gate, while a new unsigned/forged in-scope event AFTER the boundary
      FAILS it.
- [ ] **Signing is provisioned everywhere before the flip.** Each of the three writer
      surfaces has a persistent signing key configured (local per-machine key, populated
      `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` CI secret, Gerrit-side service key) and has produced at least
      one steady-state signed event confirmed via `rebar verify-identity` — done and
      confirmed BEFORE `require_authenticated` is set true.
- [ ] **The flip is atomic and enforcing.** `identity.require_authenticated = true` and the
      pinned `enforce_since` land in a single change; after it, `verify-identity`
      demonstrably FAILS on a new in-scope unsigned/forged event (enforcement is real, not
      advisory).
- [ ] **End-state:** the epic's five child stories are all in status `closed`, and each of
      their changes is present on `origin/main` (verifiable via `rebar show` per child +
      `git log origin/main`).
